content: Stress is a natural bodily response triggered by the hypothalamus, which releases CR (corticotropin-releasing hormone) that activates the pituitary gland to release cortisol.
Cortisol is essential for raising blood pressure and providing energy during peak performance, but chronic exposure can lead to oxidative damage and various diseases.
Increasing omega-3 fatty acid intake, particularly through fish oil supplements, can help lower cortisol levels and reduce inflammation associated with stress.
A 2003 study by the Diabetes Metabolism Society found that participants who took 7.2 grams of fish oil daily had significantly lower serum cortisol levels in response to mental stress.
